For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Malformed Repair Fixtures model · Theory Malform…, keep Malformed Repair Fixtures connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test misnesting, omitted elements, tables, templates, foreign content, duplicate attributes, and foster parenting.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Malformed Repair Fixtures model · Theory Malform…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Malformed Repair Fixtures precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Malformed Repair Fixtures model · Theory Malform…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 2289 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove Cheerio loading modes, malformed tree repair, parser differentials, budgets, and source-tree distinctions; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test misnesting, omitted elements, tables, templates, foreign content, duplicate attributes, and foster parenting.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Malformed Repair Fixtures model · Theory Malform…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Malformed HTML fails instead of producing a repaired tree.
